I say: There is no harm in this narration, and it interprets "guardianship" (الولاية) in the verses as the guardianship of love and affection, and the support for this has already preceded. If it were a true occasion of revelation, the verses are absolute and apply to other than this story just as they were revealed and applied within it. If it is a case of "running" (الجري) and application (التطبيق), then the matter is even clearer.

In *al-Majma'*, regarding His saying, the Exalted: **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَنْ يَرْتَدَّ مِنْكُمْ عَنْ دِينِهِ فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِي اللهُ بِقَوْمٍ** (the verse), it is said: "It is said they are the Commander of the Faithful, 'Ali (عليه السلام), and his companions when he fought those who fought him: the Breakers of the Covenant (الناكثين), the Deviators (القاسطين), and the Renegades (المارقين). This was narrated from 'Ammar, Hudhayfah, and Ibn 'Abbas, and it is what is narrated from Abu Ja'far and Abu 'Abdillah (عليهما السلام)."

I say: The author of *al-Majma'* said, after mentioning the narration: "This view is supported by the fact that the Prophet described him with these attributes mentioned in the verse. He said regarding him when he summoned him for the conquest of Khaybar—after the standard-bearer had returned to him time after time, making the people cowards and they making him a coward—: 'Tomorrow I shall certainly give the standard to a man who loves Allah and His Messenger and whom Allah and His Messenger love; a constant attacker, never a fleer, who will not return until Allah grants victory at his hand.' Then he gave it to him."

As for the description of being gentle toward the people of faith, severe against the disbelievers, and striving in the way of Allah while not fearing the blame of any blamer, no one can deny 'Ali (عليه السلام) his entitlement to that, given what appeared of his severity against the people of polytheism and disbelief, his impact upon them, and his famous positions in consolidating the creed, helping the religion, and compassion for the believers.

This is also supported by the warning of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) to the Quraysh regarding 'Ali (عليه السلام) fighting them after him. This was when Suhayl ibn 'Amr came with a group of them and said: "O Muhammad, our slaves have joined you, so return them to us." The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "You shall certainly desist, O community of Quraysh, or Allah will certainly send against you a man who will strike you for the interpretation (تأويل) of the Quran just as I struck you for its revelation (تنزيل)." Some of his companions asked: "Who is he, O Messenger of Allah? Abu Bakr?" He said: "No, but he is the one mending the sandal in the room." And 'Ali (عليه السلام) was mending the sandal of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم).

It is narrated from 'Ali (عليه السلام) that he said on the day of Basra: "By Allah, the people of this verse have not been fought until today," and he recited this verse.

Abu Ishaq al-Tha'labi narrated in his *Tafsir* with a chain of transmission from al-Zuhri, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, from Abu Hurayrah, that the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "A group of my companions will be brought to me on the Day of Resurrection and will be turned away from the Pond (الحوض). I will say: 'O Lord, my companions, my companions!' It will be said: 'You do not know what they innovated after you; they turned back on their heels in apostasy (القهقرى).'" End quote.

What he mentioned is only fully realized in him (عليه السلام), and there is no doubt that he is the best instance (مصداق) of the attributes listed in the verse. However, the issue lies in the application of the verse to the generality of those who were with him at the battles of the Camel and Siffin, for many of them changed after that. The saying of the Exalted: **يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُ** (etc.) occurred in the verse without exception, and you have already learned its meaning.

In the same source: It is narrated that the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) was asked about this verse, so he struck his hand on the shoulder of Salman and said: "This one and his people." Then he said: "If the religion were suspended at the Pleiades, men from the sons of Persia would surely reach it."

I say: The discussion regarding this is like the discussion regarding the previous one, unless it is meant that they will be sent from his people.

In the same source: It is said they are the people of Yemen; they are the ones with the gentlest hearts and the softest souls. "Faith is Yemeni, and wisdom is Yemeni." 'Iyad ibn Ghanam al-Ash'ari said: "When this verse was revealed, the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) pointed to Abu Musa al-Ash'ari and said: 'They are the people of this man.'"

I say: This meaning is narrated in *al-Durr al-Manthur* through several paths, and the discussion regarding it is like the discussion regarding the previous one.

In the *Tafsir* of al-Tabari, with his chain of transmission from Qatadah, who said: "Allah revealed this verse knowing that apostates would apostatize from among the people. When Allah took His Prophet Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), the generality of the Arabs apostatized from Islam, except for three mosques: the people of Medina, the people of Mecca, and the people of Bahrain. They said: 'We will pray but we will not pay zakat; by Allah, our wealth shall not be usurped.' Abu Bakr was spoken to regarding this, and it was said to them: 'If they understood this, they would give it and more.' He said: 'No, by Allah, I will not separate between things that Allah has joined. If they withhold even a hobbling-cord (عقال) of what Allah and His Messenger made obligatory, I would fight them for it.' So Allah sent a band with Abu Bakr, and he fought for what the Prophet of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) fought for, until he took captives, killed, and burned with fire people who had apostatized from Islam and withheld the zakat. He fought them until they submitted to the 'utensil' (الماعون)—which is the zakat—in humble submission." (The narration).

I say: It was also narrated in *al-Durr al-Manthur* from 'Abd ibn Humayd, Ibn Jarir, Ibn al-Mundhir, Abu al-Shaykh, al-Bayhaqi, and Ibn 'Asakir from Qatadah; and it was also narrated from al-Dahhak and al-Hasan.

The wording of the narration is the clearest witness that it is of the nature of theoretical application (تطبيق نظري). At that point, the same objections directed at the previous narrations apply to it. For these events and expeditions included incidents and matters where men like Khalid, Mughirah ibn Shu'bah, Busr ibn Artat, and Samurah ibn Jundab fought; history records from them during and after that such injustices and sins that do not allow the verse (**يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُ**, etc.) to be true of them or apply to them. You must return to history and then reflect upon what we have presented regarding the meaning of the verse.

The excess of some commentators reached the point that they found strange the saying of some: "The verse applies more clearly to the Ash'arites of the people of Yemen than to those who fought the people of apostasy." He said: "The verse is general, including everyone who helped the religion from among those characterized by its content—from the best of the Muslims among the believers of the Prophet's (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) era, and those believers who came after that. It applies to all the aforementioned reports, such as the report indicating they are Salman and his people—despite its weakness—the report indicating it is Abu Musa al-Ash'ari and his people, and the report indicating it is Abu Bakr and his companions. Except for what indicates it is 'Ali (عليه السلام), for the wording of the verse does not apply to him because the word 'people' (قوم)—used in the verse—does not apply to an individual, as it is explicit in referring to a group."

This is the summary of his words. It is nothing other than that he treated the speech of the Exalted—in what He mentioned of praise and commendation for the people—as one treats poetry, which builds praise upon imagination. Whatever the poet's imagination can muster, he attributes to his subject without regard for truth or falsehood. Yet Allah, the Exalted, has said: **وَمَنْ أَصْدَقُ مِنَ اللهِ قِيلاً** "And who is more truthful in speech than Allah?" (4:122). Or he treated it according to the customary speech current among us, which relies in its delivery only upon understandings built on indulgence and laxity in receiving and delivering, and excusing indulgence in everything that is problematic. Yet Allah, the Exalted, has said: **إِنَّهُ لَقَوْلٌ فَصْلٌ وَما هُوَ بِالْهَزْلِ** "It is indeed a decisive word, and it is not a jest." (86:13-14). You have already learned that if the verse were given its true meaning regarding the attributes it contains, it would become clear that its instance (مصداق) has not yet been realized until this time. Refer back, reflect, and then judge as you will.

What he mentioned at the end of his words is strange, for those who mentioned the revelation of the verse concerning 'Ali (عليه السلام) only mentioned 'Ali and his companions, just as others mentioned Salman and his kin, others Abu Musa and his people, and others Abu Bakr and his companions. Similarly, the narrations that have been transmitted—some of which have preceded—were only transmitted regarding 'Ali and his companions. No one mentioned the revelation of the verse concerning 'Ali (عليه السلام) alone such that it could be objected that the wording of the verse is explicit in referring to a group and does not apply to an individual.

Yes, it was narrated in the *Tafsir* of al-Tha'labi that it was revealed concerning 'Ali, and also in *Nahj al-Bayan* by al-Shaybani from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (عليه السلام) that it was revealed concerning 'Ali (عليه السلام). What is meant, by the evidence of other narrations, is its revelation concerning him and his companions due to their standing to help the religion in the battles of the Camel, Siffin, and against the Kharijites.

Furthermore, it will come that narrations from the paths of the general public (the Sunnis) are numerous regarding the revelation of the verse: **إِنَّما وَلِيُّكُمُ اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ** concerning 'Ali (عليه السلام), and the wording of that verse is also in the plural.

Moreover, in the narration—the narration of Qatadah, al-Dahhak, and al-Hasan—there is another problem: His saying, the Exalted: **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَنْ يَرْتَدَّ مِنْكُمْ عَنْ دِينِهِ فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِي اللهُ بِقَوْمٍ يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُ** (etc.) is apparent with an undeniable clarity in the meaning of replacement (التبديل) and dispensability (الاستغناء), whether the address is to those present on the day of revelation or to the collective of those present and those not yet born. The purpose is to address the group of believers that all or some of them, if they apostatize from their religion, Allah will replace them with a people whom He loves and who love Him—while He does not love the apostates nor do they love Him—and they possess such and such attributes and will help His religion.

This is explicit in that the "brought people" are a group of believers other than the group present at the time of revelation. Those who fought the people of apostasy shortly after the death of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) were present at the time of revelation, addressed by His saying: **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا** (etc.). Therefore, they are not the ones intended by His saying: **فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِي اللهُ بِقَوْمٍ** (etc.).

The verse follows the course of His saying, the Exalted: **وَإِنَّ تَتَوَلَّوْا يَسْتَبْدِلْ قَوْماً غَيْرَكُمْ ثُمَّ لا يَكُونُوا أَمْثالَكُمْ** "and if you turn away, He will replace you with another people, and they will not be like you." (47:38).

In the *Tafsir* of al-Nu'mani, with his chain of transmission from Sulayman ibn Harun al-'Ajli, who said: "I heard Abu 'Abdillah (عليه السلام) saying: 'The Master of this Affair (صاحب هذا الأمر) is preserved for it. If all the people were to go away, Allah would bring his companions, and they are those about whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: **فإِنْ يَكْفُرْ بِها هؤُلاءِ فَقَدْ وَكَّلْنا بِها قَوْماً لَيْسُوا بِها بِكافِرِينَ** "So if these disbelieve in them, We have certainly entrusted them to a people who do not disbelieve in them" (6:89). And they are those about whom Allah said: **فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِي اللهُ بِقَوْمٍ يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُ أَذِلَّةٍ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَعِزَّةٍ عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ**.'"

I say: al-'Ayyashi and al-Qummi narrated this meaning in their respective commentaries.

## (A Combined Discourse of Quran and Hadith)

It has been repeatedly hinted at in previous discussions that the Quranic addresses which the Quran emphasizes, and in which it intensifies its speech, do not lack a tone indicating that the existing factors and causes are cooperating to lead the people toward the depths of fall and the levels of ruin, and to the affliction of Allah's wrath—as in the verses on usury (الربا), the verse of "love for the near of kin" (مودة القربى), and others.

This is the nature of such address. For a wise speaker, if he commands a trivial and easy matter and then emphasizes it and insists upon it in a way that is not appropriate for its stature, or addresses someone with a speech that is not appropriate for such a person to receive—like forbidding a God-fearing scholar, established in asceticism and worship, from committing the most scandalous vice in public—it indicates that the case is not devoid of something deeper, and that there is a grave matter and a dangerous, looming destruction.

The Quranic addresses of this nature were followed by events that confirmed what they were hinting at, or rather, what they were indicating—even if the listeners (perhaps) did not notice, when they first heard them on the day of revelation, the signals and indications they contained.

The Quran commanded love for the near of kin of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and emphasized it until it counted it as the reward for the message and the way to Allah, the Glorified. Then it happened that the nation permitted such horrific injustices against his household that, had they been commanded to do so, they could not have added more to what they committed against them.

The Quran forbade division (الاختلاف) and emphasized it to the utmost degree. Then it happened that the nation divided and split into branches exceeding those of the Jews and Christians. The Jews were seventy-one sects, and the Christians were seventy-two sects, while the Muslims came with seventy-three sects. This is in their schools of thought regarding the intellectual knowledge of religion; as for their schools regarding social norms, the establishment of governments, and others, they do not stop at any limiting boundary.

The Quran forbade judging by other than what Allah revealed, forbade sowing division between classes, forbade tyranny (الطغيان) and following whims (اتباع الهوى), and so forth, and intensified these prohibitions. Then what happened, happened.

The matter of the prohibition of the guardianship (ولاية) of the disbelievers and the People of the Book is similar to other emphasized prohibitions in the Noble Quran. Indeed, it is not far-fetched to claim that the intensification occurring in the prohibition of the guardianship of the disbelievers and the People of the Book is not equaled by any intensification in other subsidiary prohibitions.

The matter reached the point that Allah, the Glorified, counted those who ally with the People of the Book and the disbelievers as being from them: **وَمَنْ يَتَوَلَّهُمْ مِنْكُمْ فَإِنَّهُ مِنْهُمْ** "Any of you who allies with them is indeed one of them." (5:51). He excluded them from Himself when He said: **وَمَنْ يَفْعَلْ ذلِكَ فَلَيْسَ مِنَ اللهِ فِي شَيْءٍ** "and whoever does that has nothing to do with Allah" (3:28). He warned them with the ultimate warning, saying time after time: **وَيُحَذِّرُكُمُ اللهُ نَفْسَهُ** "Allah warns you to beware of [disobeying] Him" (3:28, 30). It has already passed in the discussion of the verse that its implication is the inevitable occurrence of the warned-against matter as a final decree that cannot be changed or averted.

If you desire more clarity on this, reflect upon His saying, the Exalted: **وَإِنَّ كُلًّا لَمَّا لَيُوَفِّيَنَّهُمْ رَبُّكَ أَعْمالَهُمْ** "Indeed your Lord will pay each of them in full for their works" (11:111)—after He mentioned before the verse the stories of the peoples of Noah, Hud, Salih, and others, then the disagreement of the Jews regarding their Book—**إِنَّهُ بِما يَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ فَاسْتَقِمْ كَما أُمِرْتَ وَمَنْ تابَ مَعَكَ وَلا تَطْغَوْا** "Indeed He is aware of what they do. So be steadfast as you have been commanded—[you] and those who have turned [to Allah] with you—and do not overstep the bounds" (11:111-112). The address, as you see, is a social address (خطاب اجتماعي). **إِنَّهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ** "Indeed He sees best what you do." Then reflect upon His saying, the Exalted, after it: **وَلا تَرْكَنُوا إِلَى الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا فَتَمَسَّكُمُ النَّارُ وَما لَكُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ مِنْ أَوْلِياءَ ثُمَّ لا تُنْصَرُونَ** "And do not incline toward the wrongdoers, lest the Fire should touch you, and you will not have any friends besides Allah, then you will not be helped." (11:113).

Allah, the Glorified, has explained the meaning of the "touching of this Fire" in this world before the Hereafter—and the verse is absolute. It is that which He threatened in His saying: **وَيُحَذِّرُكُمُ اللهُ نَفْسَهُ** by His saying, the Exalted: **الْيَوْمَ يَئِسَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ دِينِكُمْ فَلا تَخْشَوْهُمْ وَاخْشَوْنِ** "Today the faithless have despaired of your religion. So do not fear them, but fear Me." (5:3). He explained therein that what the believers used to fear for their religion from those who disbelieved—who were the polytheists and the People of the Book, as explained previously—until the day this verse was revealed, they are today in safety from it. Thus, it is not appropriate for them to fear them regarding it; rather, they must fear their Lord regarding it. What they used to fear from them for their religion was that the disbelievers had no concern regarding them except to extinguish the light of the religion and to strip this precious commodity from their hands by whatever means they could.

This is what they feared before today. With the revelation of Surah al-Ma'idah, they became safe from that and their souls were at peace. However, they must fear their Lord in that regard—that He might take away their light or strip them of His religion.

It is well known that Allah, the Glorified, does not surprise a people with vengeance or punishment without their deserving it. He, the Exalted, said: **ذلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللهَ لَمْ يَكُ مُغَيِّراً نِعْمَةً أَنْعَمَها عَلى قَوْمٍ حَتَّى يُغَيِّرُوا ما بِأَنْفُسِهِمْ** "That is because Allah never changes a blessing He has bestowed on a people unless they change what is in their own souls" (8:53). He explained that His changing of a blessing only occurs due to desert, and that it follows the people's changing of what is in their souls. He has called the religion or the religious guardianship (الولاية الدينية) a "blessing" (نعمة), as He said after it: **الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ وَأَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِي وَرَضِيتُ لَكُمُ الْإِسْلامَ دِيناً** "Today I have perfected your religion for you, and I have completed My blessing upon you, and I am pleased with Islam as your religion." (5:3).

Thus, the changing of this blessing from their side, the stepping away from the guardianship of Allah by severing the bond with Him, the inclining toward the wrongdoers, and the guardianship of the disbelievers and the People of the Book is what is expected of them. It is what they must fear for themselves, fearing Allah in a wrath that none can repel. He has warned them of it in His saying: **وَمَنْ يَتَوَلَّهُمْ مِنْكُمْ فَإِنَّهُ مِنْهُمْ إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ** "Any of you who allies with them is indeed one of them. Indeed Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot." (5:51). He informed that He does not guide them to their happiness (سعادتهم), for that is what guidance relates to. Their happiness in this world is only that they live according to the norm of the religion and the general Islamic way of life in their society.

When the structure of this way of life is demolished, its manifestations that preserve its meaning—such as bidding what is right (الأمر بالمعروف) and forbidding what is wrong (النهي عن المنكر)—become disordered. Its general rites fall away, and the way of life of the disbelievers takes their place, its pillars continually strengthening and its foundations becoming established. This is the state of the Muslim society today.

If you were to reflect upon the general Islamic way of life organized and established by the Book and the Sunnah among the Muslims, and then upon this corrupt way of life that has been imposed today upon the Muslims; and then reflect upon what He hints at in His saying: **فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِي اللهُ بِقَوْمٍ يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُ أَذِلَّةٍ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَعِزَّةٍ عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ يُجاهِدُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ وَلا يَخافُونَ لَوْمَةَ لائِمٍ** "Allah will soon bring a people whom He loves and who love Him, [who will be] lowly towards the faithful, mighty against the faithless, waging jihad in the way of Allah, and not fearing the blame of any blamer" (5:54), you would find that all the vices (الرذائل) that surround our society—we the community of Muslims—and rule over us today (which we have borrowed from the disbelievers and which have then grown and multiplied among us) are the opposites of what Allah mentioned in the description of those He promised to bring in the verse. That is, all our actual vices are summarized in the fact that the society today does not love Allah nor does Allah love them; they are lowly toward the disbelievers, mighty against the believers, they do not strive in the way of Allah, and they fear every blame.

This is what the Quran discerned in the face of the people. Or if you wish, say: it is the news of the Unseen (النبأ الغيبي) which the All-Knowing, the All-Aware foretold—that the Islamic society would apostatize from its religion. This is not a technical apostasy (ردة مصطلحة), but rather a metaphorical apostasy (ردة تنزيلية) explained by His saying, the Exalted: **وَمَنْ يَتَوَلَّهُمْ مِنْكُمْ فَإِنَّهُ مِنْهُمْ إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ** "Any of you who allies with them is indeed one of them. Indeed Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot" (5:51); and His saying: **وَلَوْ كانُوا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَالنَّبِيِّ وَما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مَا اتَّخَذُوهُمْ أَوْلِياءَ وَلكِنَّ كَثِيراً مِنْهُمْ فاسِقُونَ** "Had they believed in Allah and the Prophet and what was sent down to him, they would not have taken them for allies, but most of them are transgressors." (5:81).

Allah promised them victory if they helped Him, and the weakening of their enemies if they did not strengthen and support them. He said: **إِنْ تَنْصُرُوا اللهَ يَنْصُرْكُمْ** "if you help Allah, He will help you" (47:7); and He said: **وَلَوْ آمَنَ أَهْلُ الْكِتابِ لَكانَ خَيْراً لَهُمْ مِنْهُمُ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَأَكْثَرُهُمُ الْفاسِقُونَ لَنْ يَضُرُّوكُمْ إِلَّا أَذىً وَإِنْ يُقاتِلُوكُمْ يُوَلُّوكُمُ الْأَدْبارَ ثُمَّ لا يُنْصَرُونَ ضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الذِّلَّةُ أَيْنَ ما ثُقِفُوا إِلَّا بِحَبْلٍ مِنَ اللهِ وَحَبْلٍ مِنَ النَّاسِ** "Had the People of the Book believed, it would surely have been better for them. Among them are faithful, but most of them are transgressors. They will never do you any harm, except for some annoyance; and if they fight you, they will turn their backs [to flee], then they will not be helped. Abasement has been stamped upon them wherever they are found, except [when they make use of] a bond from Allah and a bond from the people." (3:110-112). It is not far-fetched to infer from His saying: **إِلَّا بِحَبْلٍ مِنَ اللهِ وَحَبْلٍ مِنَ النَّاسِ** "except [when they make use of] a bond from Allah and a bond from the people" that they may emerge from abasement and misery through the people's alliance with them and Allah's giving them power over the people.

Then Allah, the Glorified, promised the Islamic society—given this state of theirs—the bringing of a people whom He loves and who love Him, lowly toward the believers, mighty against the disbelievers, striving in the way of Allah, not fearing the blame of any blamer. The attributes listed for them—as you have seen—are the sum of the attributes that the Islamic society lacks today. By deep reflection upon them, one can infer the details of the vices that the verse foretells the Islamic society will be afflicted with.

A number of reports concerning the prophecies of the Unseen (ملاحم) of the end of times, narrated from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and the Imams from his household (عليه السلام), have included an enumeration of these. Despite their large number and the fact that, as a whole, they are not free from the blight of fabrication and distortion, there are among them reports confirmed by the course of events and the succession of external realities. These are reports taken from the books of the ancients compiled more than a thousand years ago, or close to it; their attribution to their authors is correct and the transmission from them is widespread.

Furthermore, they speak of events and occurrences that had not happened nor occurred in those times, nor were they expected or anticipated by the souls living in those ages. Thus, we have no choice but to recognize their authenticity and their issuance from the source of revelation.

As al-Qummi narrated in his *Tafsir* from his father, from Sulayman ibn Muslim al-Khashshab, from 'Abdullah ibn Jurayh al-Makki, from 'Ata' ibn Abi Rabah, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Abbas, who said: "We performed the Farewell Pilgrimage with the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). He took hold of the door of the Kaaba, then turned his face toward us and said: 'Shall I not inform you of the signs of the Hour?' The closest of people to him that day was Salman (رضي الله عنه). He said: 'Yes, O Messenger of Allah.'

He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Among the signs of the Resurrection are the wasting of prayer, the following of desires, the inclination toward whims, the veneration of wealth, and the selling of religion for the world. At that time, the heart of the believer and his interior will melt—just as salt melts in water—because of the evil (المنكر) he sees and is unable to change.'

Salman said: 'Will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?' He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Yes, by the One in Whose hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, they will be ruled by tyrannical leaders, profligate ministers, unjust officials, and treacherous trustees.'

Salman said: 'Will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?' He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Yes, by the One in Whose hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, evil will be seen as good and good as evil; the treacherous will be trusted and the trustworthy will be betrayed; the liar will be believed and the truthful will be belied.'

Salman said: 'Will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?' He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Yes, by the One in Whose hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, there will be the rule of women, the consultation of female slaves, and children sitting upon the pulpits. Lying will be considered cleverness, zakat a loss, and public wealth (الفيء) a gain. A man will be harsh toward his parents and kind to his friend, and the comet will appear.'

Salman said: 'Will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?' He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Yes, by the One in Whose hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, the woman will partner with her husband in trade. Rain will come in the heat of summer, and the noble will be deeply provoked. The destitute man will be despised. At that time, markets will draw near; when this one says, "I sold nothing," and that one says, "I made no profit," you will see none but those who blame Allah.'

Salman said: 'Will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?' He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Yes, by the One in Whose hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, they will be ruled by peoples who, if they speak, they kill them, and if they remain silent, they exploit them—to monopolize their wealth, violate their sanctity, shed their blood, and fill their hearts with terror. You will see them only as fearful, terrified, and intimidated.'

Salman said: 'Will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?' He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Yes, by the One in Whose hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, something will be brought from the East and something from the West to rule my nation. Woe to the weak of my nation from them, and woe to them from Allah! They will not show mercy to the young, nor respect the old, nor overlook the wrongdoer. Their reports will be obscene. Their bodies will be the bodies of humans, but their hearts will be the hearts of devils.'

Salman said: 'Will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?' He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Yes, by the One in Whose hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, men will be satisfied with men and women with women. Young boys will be lusted after as a young girl is lusted after in her family's house. Men will resemble women and women will resemble men. Women will ride upon saddles—upon them from my nation is the curse of Allah.'

Salman said: 'Will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?' He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Yes, by the One in Whose hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, mosques will be decorated as synagogues and churches are decorated. The Qurans will be adorned, and minarets will be tall. Ranks will be many, but with hearts that hate one another and tongues that differ.'

Salman said: 'Will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?' He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Yes, by the One in Whose hand is my soul! At that time, the males of my nation will adorn themselves with gold, wear silk and brocade, and take leopard skins as coverings.'

Salman said: 'Will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?' He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Yes, by the One in Whose hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, usury (الربا) will appear, and they will deal in backbiting and bribes. Religion will be lowered and the world will be raised.'

Salman said: 'Will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?' He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Yes, by the One in Whose hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, divorce will increase, so no legal punishment (حد) of Allah will be established, yet they will not harm Allah in the least.'

Salman said: 'Will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?' He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Yes, by the One in Whose hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, female singers and musical instruments will appear, and the evilest of my nation will rule over them.'

Salman said: 'Will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?' He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Yes, by the One in Whose hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, the wealthy of my nation will perform the Hajj for tourism, the middle class for trade, and the poor for show and reputation. At that time, there will be peoples who learn the Quran for other than Allah and take it as melodies (مزامير). There will be peoples who study religious jurisprudence (يتفقهون) for other than Allah. Children of adultery will increase, they will sing the Quran, and they will rush headlong into the world.'

Salman said: 'Will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah?' He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Yes, by the One in Whose hand is my soul, O Salman! That is when sanctities are violated, sins are earned, and the evil are given power over the good. Lying will spread, stubbornness will appear, and poverty will spread. They will boast in clothing, and it will rain out of season. They will find the drum and musical instruments pleasing. They will reject bidding what is right and forbidding what is wrong, until the believer in that time is the lowliest in the nation. Their reciters and worshippers will manifest mutual blame among themselves; those shall be called in the kingdom of the heavens: the filthy and the impure.'"

Salman asked: "Will this truly come to pass, O Messenger of Allah?" He (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) replied: "Yes, by Him in Whose hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, the wealthy will fear nothing but poverty, to the extent that a beggar will ask between two Fridays and find no one to place anything in his hand."

Salman asked: "Will this truly come to pass, O Messenger of Allah?" He (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) replied: "Yes, by Him in Whose hand is my soul, O Salman! At that time, the *ruwaybidah* (الرويبضة) will speak." Salman asked: "And what is the *ruwaybidah*, O Messenger of Allah, may my father and mother be sacrificed for you?"

He (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) replied: "Those who never used to speak will speak regarding the affairs of the masses. They will remain but a little while until the earth let out a great moan (خورة)—such that every people will think it moaned only in their region. They will remain as long as Allah wills, and then they will be shaken in their stay. Then the earth will cast out the pieces of its liver (أفلاذ كبدها)—meaning gold and silver." He then gestured with his hand toward the pillars (الأساطين) and said: "Like these. On 그날, neither gold nor silver will be of any benefit. This is the meaning of His saying: **فَقَدْ جاءَ أَشْراطُها** (for its [the Hour's] conditions have already come)."

In *Rawdat al-Kafi*, narrated from Muhammad ibn Yahya, from Ahmad ibn Muhammad, from some of his companions, and Ali ibn Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abi Umayr, all from Muhammad ibn Abi Hamzah, from Humran, who said: Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) said—mentioning those [the Abbasids] in his presence and the poor state of the Shi‘a under them:

"I was traveling with Abu Ja‘far al-Mansur while he was in his procession. He was on a horse, with cavalry before him and cavalry behind him, while I was on a donkey by his side. He said to me: 'O Abu Abdillah, it would befit you to rejoice in the power Allah has given us and the glory He has opened for us. Do not tell the people that you and your household are more entitled to this matter than we are, lest you incite us against yourself and them.'"

He [the Imam] said: "I replied: 'Whoever reported this to you on my behalf has lied.' He said: 'Will you swear to what you say?' I said: 'Indeed, people are sorcerers (سحرة)—meaning they love to corrupt your heart against me—so do not give them your ear, for we are in greater need of you than you are of us.' He then said to me: 'Do you remember the day I asked you: "Shall we have a kingdom?" and you said: "Yes—long, wide, and mighty. You shall remain in a period of respite in your affair and plenty in your worldly life until you strike from us sacred blood, in a sacred month, in a sacred land"?' I realized then that he had memorized the tradition (الحديث). I said: 'Perhaps Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will suffice you—for I did not specify you with this; it is merely a tradition I narrated. Furthermore, perhaps someone else from your household will take charge of that.' So he remained silent toward me."

"When I returned to my home, one of our followers (موالينا) came to me and said: 'May I be sacrificed for you! By Allah, I saw you in the procession of Abu Ja‘far while you were on a donkey and he was on a horse. He was looking down upon you, speaking to you as if you were beneath him. I said to myself: "This is the Proof of Allah (حجة الله) over creation and the master of this affair who is to be followed, while this other one acts with injustice (الجور), kills the children of the Prophets, and sheds blood on earth in a way Allah does not love—yet he is in his procession while you are on a donkey!" Doubt entered me because of that, until I feared for my religion and my soul.'"

He (peace be upon him) said: "I told him: 'Had you seen the angels who were around me—before me, behind me, on my right, and on my left—you would have looked down upon him and looked down upon the state he was in.' He said: 'Now my heart is at peace.'"

Then he asked: "How long will these people rule, or when will there be relief from them?" I said: "Do you not know that for everything there is a term?" He said: "Yes." I said: "Will it benefit you to know that when this matter comes, it will be faster than the twinkling of an eye? If you knew their state before Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and how it truly is, you would hate them even more. Even if you and all the inhabitants of the earth exerted every effort to plunge them into more sin than they are already in, you would not be able to. So do not let Satan provoke you—for might belongs to Allah, His Messenger, and the believers, but the hypocrites do not know. Do you not know that whoever awaits our affair and is patient with the harm and fear he sees, shall be in our company tomorrow?

When you see that the truth (الحق) has died and its people have departed; when you see that injustice (الجور) has encompassed the lands; when you see that the Quran has become worn out (خلق)—and things not in it are innovated within it and it is directed according to whims (الأهواء); when you see that the religion (الدين) has been overturned as a vessel is overturned; when you see the people of falsehood (أهل الباطل) have risen above the people of truth; when you see evil (الشر) manifest, not being forbidden, and its practitioners excused; when you see that immorality (الفسق) has appeared—men sufficing themselves with men and women with women; when you see the believer silent, his word not accepted; when you see the profligate lying, yet his lie and slander are not refuted; when you see the young belittling the old; when you see that ties of kinship (الأرحام) have been severed; when you see the one who boasts of immorality being laughed with, his words not rebuffed; when you see the youth being given what a woman is given; when you see women marrying women; when you see that praise has increased; when you see a man spending wealth in other than the obedience of Allah—yet he is not forbidden nor is his hand stayed; when you see the onlooker seeking refuge with Allah from the exertion (الاجتهاد) he sees the believer in; when you see a neighbor harming his neighbor with no one to stop him; when you see the disbeliever (الكافر) joyful at what he sees in the believer, exultant at the corruption (الفساد) he sees on earth; when you see intoxicants (الخمور) drunk openly—and those who do not fear Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, gathering for it; when you see 'enjoining the good' (الأمر بالمعروف) humiliated; when you see the profligate strong and praised in what Allah does not love; when you see the people of the traditions (أصحاب الآثار) belittled, and those who love them belittled; when you see the path of good (سبيل الخير) cut off and the path of evil (سبيل الشر) trodden; when you see the House of Allah [the Kaaba] neglected and its abandonment commanded; when you see a man saying what he does not do; when you see men lusting after men and women after women; when you see a man's livelihood coming from his posterior and a woman's livelihood from her private parts; when you see women taking up assemblies as men do; when you see effeminacy (التأنيث) appearing among the children of al-Abbas—they manifest the use of dye (الخضاب) and comb their hair as a woman combs it for her husband, giving men wealth for their private parts, and men competing for a man and being jealous over him; when the owner of wealth is more honored than the believer; when usury (الربا) is manifest and not reproached; when women are praised for adultery (الزنا); when you see a woman facilitating her husband's intercourse with men; when you see that most people and the 'best' of houses are those that assist women in their immorality; when you see the believer sorrowful, belittled, and humiliated; when you see innovations (البدع) and adultery have appeared; when you see people relying on false testimony; when you see the forbidden (الحرام) made permissible and the permissible (الحلال) made forbidden; when you see religion practiced by personal opinion (الرأي) and the Book and its rulings neglected; when you see that the night is no longer used for concealment out of audacity toward Allah; when you see the believer unable to denounce except with his heart; when you see vast amounts of wealth spent in the displeasure of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic; when you see the rulers (الولاة) drawing the people of disbelief close and distancing the people of good; when you see the rulers taking bribes in judgment; when you see governorship (الولاية) given to the highest bidder; when you see women of prohibited degrees (ذوات الأرحام) being married and sufficing; when you see a man killed on mere accusation and suspicion; when you see a man being jealous over another male, offering him his person and wealth; when you see a man reproached for approaching women; when you see a man eating from the immoral earnings of his wife—knowing it and persisting in it; when you see a woman overpowering her husband, doing what he does not desire and spending on her husband; when you see a man renting out his wife and slave-girl, being satisfied with base food and drink; when you see oaths (الأيمان) by Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, frequently made in falsehood; when you see gambling (القمار) has appeared; when you see intoxicants sold openly with no one to stop it; when you see women offering themselves to the people of disbelief; when you see places of amusement (الملاهي) have appeared, passed by with no one forbidding anyone, nor anyone daring to stop them; when you see the noble person humiliated by the one whose authority he fears; when you see the closest people to the rulers are those who boast of reviling us, the People of the House (أهل البيت); when you see those who love us being falsified and their testimony not accepted; when you see false speech (الزور) being competed for; when you see the Quran has become heavy for people to listen to, while listening to falsehood (الباطل) has become light; when you see a neighbor honoring a neighbor out of fear of his tongue; when you see the prescribed punishments (الحدود) neglected and whims (الأهواء) acted upon; when you see mosques (المساجد) decorated; when you see the most truthful of people in the eyes of the people is the one who fabricates lies; when you see evil has appeared and tale-bearing (النميمة) is rampant; when you see transgression (البغي) has spread; when you see backbiting (الغيبة) found pleasant, with people giving each other glad tidings of it; when you see the seeking of Hajj and Jihad for other than Allah; when you see the ruler (السلطان) humiliating the believer for the sake of the disbeliever; when you see ruin (الخراب) has taken the place of cultivation (العمران); when you see a man's livelihood coming from cheating the measure and the scale; when you see the shedding of blood taken lightly; when you see a man seeking leadership (الرئاسة) for worldly purposes, making himself known for a foul tongue so that he may be feared and matters referred to him; when you see prayer (الصلاة) taken lightly; when you see a man possessing much wealth yet not purifying it [paying Zakat] since he owned it; when you see the dead exhumed from their graves, harmed, and their shrouds sold; when you see turmoil (الهرج) has increased; when you see a man spending his evening tipsy and waking up drunk, unconcerned with the state of the people; when you see beasts being copulated with; when you see beasts preying on one another; when you see a man going out to his place of prayer and returning without any of his clothes on him; when you see the hearts of people have hardened, their eyes have dried, and remembrance (الذكر) has become heavy upon them; when you see illicit gain (السحت) has appeared and is competed for; when you see the one who prays doing so only to be seen by people; when you see the jurist (الفقيه) studying for other than religion—seeking the world and leadership; when you see the people are with whoever prevails; when you see the seeker of the permissible (الحلال) blamed and reproached, while the seeker of the forbidden (الحرام) is praised and magnified; when you see the Two Sanctuaries [Mecca and Medina] being used for what Allah does not love—with no one to stop them or stand between them and the ugly deed; when you see musical instruments (المعازف) manifest in the Two Sanctuaries; when you see a man speaking something of the truth, enjoining the good and forbidding the evil, only for someone to stand and 'advise' him regarding himself, saying: 'This is not your responsibility'; when you see people looking at one another and following the example of the people of evil; when you see the path of good and its way empty, with no one walking it; when you see the dead being mocked, with no one alarmed by it; when you see every year innovations (البدع) and evil occurring more than before; when you see the creation and the assemblies following none but the wealthy; when you see the needy being given only to be laughed at, and pitied for other than the sake of Allah; when you see signs (الآيات) in the sky yet no one is alarmed; when you see people copulating as beasts copulate—with no one denouncing any evil out of fear of the people; when you see a man spending much in other than the obedience of Allah, yet withholding a small amount in the obedience of Allah; when you see disobedience to parents (العقوق) has appeared and they are belittled—being in the worst state with the child, who rejoices in slandering them; when you see women have overcome the kingdom and overcome every matter, such that nothing is done except what they desire; when you see a man's son slandering his father, praying against his parents, and rejoicing at their death; when you see a man, if a day passes without him committing a great sin—be it immorality, cheating the measure or scale, approaching the forbidden, or drinking an intoxicant—he becomes dejected and sorrowful, considering that day a loss from his life; when you see the ruler (السلطان) hoarding food; when you see the wealth of the near of kin (ذوي القربى) divided in falsehood, gambled with, and used to drink intoxicants; when you see wine (الخمر) used as medicine, prescribed for the sick, and sought for healing; when you see people have become equal in abandoning 'enjoining the good' and 'forbidding the evil' and abandoning practicing religion through it; when you see the winds of the hypocrites and the people of hypocrisy (أهل النفاق) are steady, while the winds of the people of truth do not move; when you see the call to prayer (الأذان) done for a wage and the prayer (الصلاة) performed for a wage; when you see the mosques filled with those who do not fear Allah—gathering there for backbiting and eating the flesh of the people of truth, and describing intoxicants therein; when you see the drunkard leading the people in prayer—he being senseless and not disgraced by his drunkenness, and when drunk he is honored, feared, and avoided, not punished but excused for his drunkenness; when you see the one who devours the wealth of orphans praised for his 'righteousness'; when you see the judges (القضاة) judging contrary to what Allah commanded; when you see the rulers (الولاة) trusting the treacherous out of greed; when you see the rulers have assigned inheritance (الميراث) to the people of immorality and those who are audacious toward Allah—taking from them and leaving them to what they desire; when you see the pulpits (المنابر) used to command God-consciousness (التقوى) while the speaker does not act upon what he commands; when you see the times of prayer (الصلاة) taken lightly; when you see charity (الصدقة) given through intercession (الشفاعة)—not intending the face of Allah, but given to satisfy people's requests; when you see the concern of people is their bellies and their private parts—not caring what they eat or whom they marry; when you see the world (الدنيا) approaching them; and when you see the banners of truth (أعلام الحق) have been obliterated—then be on your guard, and seek salvation from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. Know that the people are under the wrath of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic; He only gives them respite for a matter intended for them. So be watchful, and strive so that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sees you in opposition to what they are upon. If the punishment descends upon them and you are among them, you will hasten to the mercy of Allah; and if you are delayed, they will be afflicted while you have exited from the audacity toward Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, that they are in. Know that Allah does not waste the reward of the virtuous, and that the mercy of Allah is near to the virtuous."

I say: There are many narrated reports from the Prophet and the Imams of his household (peace be upon them) regarding these meanings. What we have quoted from these two traditions is among the most comprehensive in meaning. These traditions (reports of the end times) serve as an elaboration of what the noble verse indicates, namely His saying, the Exalted: **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَنْ يَرْتَدَّ مِنْكُمْ عَنْ دِينِهِ فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِي اللهُ بِقَوْمٍ يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُ أَذِلَّةٍ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَعِزَّةٍ عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ يُجاهِدُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ وَلا يَخافُونَ لَوْمَةَ لائِمٍ** (O you who have faith! Should any of you desert his religion, Allah will soon bring a people whom He loves and who love Him, [who will be] humble towards the believers, mighty against the faithless, waging jihad in the way of Allah, not fearing the blame of any blamer) [5:54]. And Allah knows best.

Completed, and praise be to Allah.
